extraction.one_line_summary: BMC won the opening TTT by 17 seconds over Quick Step Floors, finishing with five riders including DENNIS Rohan who took the leader's jersey.
tactical_patterns: BMC only fifth at intermediate check but still won by 17s, revealing dangerous late pacing over flat finishing kilometers.
Quick Step Floors dropped rider(s) late despite clean intermediate, suggesting their depth vulnerable when pace sustained over 20+ minutes.
Sky suffered catastrophic wheel failure mid-effort, indicating equipment gambles under team time trial power loads risky even for top teams.

rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: Crossed line first for BMC despite team fifth at intermediate, demonstrating superior final kilometers power management
rider_signals.BONNET William: negative: Visibly furious with ROY Jérémy during FDJ effort, exposing internal coordination problems under sustained TTT pace
rider_signals.OSS Daniel: positive: Finished with BMC's winning five-rider group, revenge-motivated after worlds defeat to Quick Step
